claims.text: The c.1935C>A (p.D645E) variant in the GAA gene is present in 7 of the 8 Pompe disease patients enrolled in this study (all except Subject A1), in both IOPD and LOPD patients.

citations.quote: Apart from Subject A1, all carried at least one allele with the c.1935C>A (p.D645E) variant in the GAA gene.

claims.text: In Table 1, the c.1935C>A variant is consistently reported in cis with c.1726G>A as the haplotype c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] across subjects A2, A3, B1, B2, B3, B4, and B5.

Subject B4 (male, IOPD, NBS-diagnosed) carries c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] on both alleles (homozygous for this haplotype), started ERT at age 0.1 years at 40 mg/kg Q2W, and developed high ADA titers at age 1.6 years after 1.5 years of ERT.

Subject B1 (female, IOPD, NBS-diagnosed) carries c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] in trans with c.[1411_1414del;752C>T;761C>T], started ERT at 0.1 years at 20 mg/kg Q2W, and developed high ADA at 7.9 years after 7.8 years of ERT.

Subject B2 (female, IOPD, NBS-diagnosed) carries c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] in trans with c.2024_2026del, started ERT at 0.02 years at 40 mg/kg Q2W, and developed high ADA at 1.4 years after 1.4 years of ERT.

Subject B3 (female, IOPD, clinical case) carries c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] in trans with c.1340_1350del, started ERT at 0.3 years at 40 mg/kg Q2W, and developed high ADA at 0.5 years after 0.2 years of ERT.

Subject B5 (female, IOPD, clinical case) carries c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] in trans with c.[1001G>A;1726G>A], started ERT at 0.3 years at 40 mg/kg QW, and developed high ADA at 0.4 years after 0.1 years of ERT.

Subject A2 (male, LOPD, clinical case) carries c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] in trans with c.1781G>A, started ERT at age 3.2 years, and developed high ADA at 9.8 years after 6.6 years of ERT; muscle weakness was noted during early adolescence.

citations.quote: Three patients were diagnosed with LOPD (Subject A1-A3) with muscle weakness noted during early adolescence, early infancy, and four years of age, respectively.

claims.text: Subject A3 (male, LOPD, clinical case) carries c.[1935C>A;1726G>A] in trans with c.[842G>T;1726G>A], started ERT at age 7 years at 40 mg/kg QW, and developed high ADA at 7.3 years after 0.3 years of ERT; muscle weakness was noted from age four years.
All patients carrying c.1935C>A were CRIM-positive, confirmed by western blotting or genotyping prediction.

citations.quote: All IOPD patients were confirmed CRIM-positive through western blotting, described previously, or genotyping prediction

claims.text: The clinical phenotypes of the c.1935C>A-carrying patients include classic IOPD (5 patients with significant cardiac complications) and LOPD (2–3 patients with muscle weakness but no cardiac manifestations); none of the LOPD patients exhibited cardiac involvement.

citations.quote: The remaining five were diagnosed with classic IOPD between 8 days and three months of age, primarily presenting with significant cardiac complications. Apart from Subject A1, all carried at least one allele with the c.1935C>A (p.D645E) variant in the GAA gene. All IOPD patients were confirmed CRIM-positive
